codeReview.summary: The returned-task panel correctly retitles and filters the UI to rejected tasks, but the implementation narrows the data source to only `status === 'rejected'` tasks and drops returned draft tasks that remain the active resubmission records after later workflow fixes.

issues.description: The list now filters exclusively with `isRejectedDesignerTask(task)`, so tasks that are returned to the designer as editable drafts (`currentNode === 'sj'`, `status === 'draft'`, `returnReason` present) disappear from the returned-task list even though they are still the resubmittable returned tasks in the workflow. This breaks the milestone's returned-list behavior once a returned task is normalized back to draft instead of staying rejected.
Test coverage only adds a rejected-task case and does not lock in the pre-existing returned-draft path for the actual returned-task panel, so the regression in editable returned tasks was not caught.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The meaning of 'returned task' is split between rejected records and designer-editable draft records with `returnReason`, and that workflow nuance is not documented in the shared library even though later fixes rely on preserving returned drafts for resubmission.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/components/review/reviewTaskFilters.ts` distinguishes `isDesignerResubmissionTask` (draft at sj with returnReason) from `isRejectedDesignerTask`, while later commit `6bafbc7` adjusts store behavior to keep returned tasks visible for resubmission. `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents currentNode/status ambiguity generally but not this specific returned-task contract.

summary: Fail: the feature's UI changes are present, but the core filter is too strict. By sourcing the panel only from `status='rejected'`, it hides returned draft tasks that remain the active resubmission items, so the returned-task list is not robust against the actual workflow state model.
